D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Laplacian of Gaussian — Mexican-Hat Edge / Blob

LoG = ∇²(G_σ ∗ I) — second-derivative response after Gaussian smoothing; zero-crossings mark edges, peaks mark blobs of radius ≈ σ√2

Marr-Hildreth (1980) showed that LoG zero-crossings are an early-vision-like edge representation. The Mexican-hat kernel approximates how retinal ganglion cells respond. Used in SIFT/SURF blob detection; modern CNN feature maps inherit this template.

σ controls scale: small σ finds fine edges, large σ finds blobs.
